MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Aug-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Jon C. James of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Hoffman Construction Co.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Maintaining the Current Trial Date with Preference is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). No opposition filed.

The trial date of October 3, 2022 is vacated. The new trial date is December 12, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 23, 2022. All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the March 28, 2022 trial date.

Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 28, 2022. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 11, 2022. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by December 2, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, December 8, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Aug-25-2022 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs/opposing parties, Dolores Sugarman, et al.
Paul D. Smith of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0339), appearing for defendant/moving party, Hofmann Construction Co.

RULING:
ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Hofmann Construction Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant fails to address testimony from Charles Willert that he and decedent would eat lunch in the houses while the drywallers were performing their work and they would pull out after a half hour if it got really dusty in there. (Smith Declaration, Ex. E at 28:25-29:14.) Mr. Willert also testified that defendant's employees would clean and sweep the houses while he and decedent were eating lunch. (Id. at 24:12-25:9.) There is no evidence that Mr. Willert was asked how he knew the materials Defendant's employees were cleaning up contained asbestos. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Not reported. (503/CML)
